turns.user_message: My chest feels heavy today, like there is a dull weight sitting right behind my ribs. It used to just be a passing thing that I could easily brush off after a long day. Now I just stare at the wall and notice how stiff my shoulders are. I tried picking up a book to distract myself, but the words just slide right past my eyes. Everything feels completely flat and I am just waiting for the feeling to pass.

That heavy sensation in my chest hasn't really lifted for a while now. It used to be something I barely noticed, but lately it just sits there like a constant anchor. I keep trying to do the usual things to shake it off, but nothing actually lands. My hands just feel numb when I reach for my favorite snacks or turn on music. I am just moving through the hours without any real spark or color.

The weight on my chest has turned into this thick, suffocating blanket that I cannot seem to shrug off. I used to just ignore it and carry on, but now it completely drains whatever energy I have left. I look around at everything I own and feel absolutely nothing, like my nerves are just disconnected. Even simple routines feel like climbing a steep hill while carrying bricks. I am just trapped in this quiet, empty space where nothing reaches me anymore.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This used to not bother the speaker.
